intro_cards.title: Before: 前に
intro_cards.body:
前に (まえに) means 'before doing ~' with a verb, or 'before [time/event]' with a noun.

With a verb (always plain non-past form):
• 寝る前に歯を磨きます。(I brush my teeth before sleeping.)
• 食べる前に手を洗います。(I wash my hands before eating.)

With a noun:
• 食事の前に (before the meal)
• 会議の前に (before the meeting)

Contrast with てから ('after'):
• 食べる前に手を洗う (wash BEFORE eating)
• 手を洗ってから食べる (eat AFTER washing)

⚠ Always use plain non-past before 前に, regardless of sentence tense.
